mnemonics: PLAIN Function: Passes the Line Assignment Test (each input has one output). ONE-to-ONE: Only Never Equals (no two different inputs produce the same output).
keyPoints: A function is a relation where each element in the domain is mapped to exactly one element in the codomain.
A one-to-one (injective) function is a function where each element in the codomain is mapped to by at most one element in the domain.
The Horizontal Line Test is a graphical way to check if a function is one-to-one: if any horizontal line intersects the graph more than once, it is not one-to-one.
commonMistakes: Confusing a plain function (which is just a standard function) with a one-to-one function. All one-to-one functions are functions, but not all functions are one-to-one.
Assuming a function is one-to-one because it passes the vertical line test. The vertical line test checks if a relation is a function, while the horizontal line test checks for one-to-one.
